review_text: I will rate a 1 star simply because I’m unable to rate a zero. Don’t get me wrong, Ross is a beautiful little gem & every other local person we spoke to in this little town were absolutely lovely. However the so called manager of this gorgeous little park is a complete nightmare.  Our first experience of her was before we’d even checked in. When we did eventually find the office she was in such a bad mood she through keys across her desk because she couldn’t find something. We should have left then but we were so tired we needed to rest. She is so condescending & rude. We have been travelling this beautiful country full time for the last 2 years & have never come across anyone like this anywhere we have stayed.  She accused me of being aggressive & threatened to kick us out simply because I asked her to leave us alone. In my opinion as long as she is in that position she is dragging the town down.

Its OK but only 2 toilets per gender is pretty slim.  Many go across to the public WC which has more.
Camp kitchen looks good,  but most things v aren't working or trips out..
One of the few that allows camping,  and at $25 is good value.

Wow, what a beautiful place Ross is and the people are awesome. Except one person we unfortunately had do deal with. The manager of the Ross caravan park would be one of the rudest, provoking, power tripping, hostile person we've dealt with in Tasmania. Threatened us before getting 5 meters on-site, told us to slow down or have you money refunded. Hard to speed when idling into the site. Manager stops us, have you checked in? Unknown to us, check in is back at the motel ,not in the caravan park itself? Wife gets out to walk back to check in when manager tells me to move forward (twice) had to tell her that I can't move until wife is out of the vehicle and door closed. Then she went on her power trip, trying to get a rise out of me. Threatens to evict us, I can do that as I'm the manager. She had no clothing to suggest or tie her to the park, just a plain black shirt. I feel sorry for the business owners and residents of this beautiful place, as her actions would be destructive to the locals. I wouldn't like to work for her, I can see her being the bullying type of employer. Ross may need to open a place to park for the night in a paddock, before the tourist stop coming.

Wonderful little caravan park. We thoroughly enjoyed our stay. The staff (especially management and maintenance) were very friendly and went out of their way to ensure we had an enjoyable stay. I highly recommend the park to anyone looking for a friendly, clean, well-run place to stay. Hopefully we will have the chance to get there again. A huge thank you to Emily for all her hard work. It didn't go unnoticed. THANK YOU!!! Kim and Jane :)

CALL FOR A BOYCOTT

Charming place, reasonable price, good location in Ross.
That cannot be taken away from it.

But what can you do when you are faced with a manager who uses her small amount of power to discriminate?

We had informed them of our arrival: three additional cars and two tents, approved in writing and verbally.
Despite this, at 10 p.m., the manager allowed herself to enter our car, flashlight on, while we were already lying in bed.
Unacceptable.

She reproached us for an alleged disagreement regarding the number of vehicles and tents. We calmly reconfirmed what had been announced and told her that, if there was any misunderstanding, we would resolve it at the office the following day.
She insisted, still with her flashlight, still while we were in our bed.
She left without even closing the door.

We could stop here, given the blatant lack of respect, basic manners, and professionalism. But no.

The next day, we all went to the office to pay. Impossible: the manager refused to move on, locked herself into bad faith, and continued to insist.
When my friend showed her the written messages confirming our agreement, she started to stutter, then claimed that “verbally, that was not what had been said.”
It was clear that she did not want things to go well. She did not want us there and openly expressed her contempt for backpackers.

Provocations, condescending looks, raised eyebrows: every attempt at dialogue was met with arrogance.
When I told her, firmly but politely, that we were responsible adults and that it was unacceptable to speak to us that way, she seized the opportunity to say that I was aggressive and insulting and immediately expelled me from the campsite.

When people full of contempt gain a little bit of power, this is what happens.

She also forbade us to smoke in the campsite. We completely understand this. But once again, the tone, the aggressiveness, and the total lack of willingness to calm the situation made any discussion impossible.
For clarification: we arrived at dusk after informing them, smoked inside our vehicle, with no music, no gathering, no disturbance, before going to work the next day.

When we returned from work, we were forced to find another place to sleep.

The icing on the cake: the manager contacted my friends’ boss, had him come to the campsite, with no legitimate connection to the situation. An obvious attempt to cause harm, perhaps even to make someone lose their job.
Hatred sometimes has no limits. It is miserable.

Once again: the place is peaceful and pleasant.
But I am calling for a boycott of this Caravan Park for anyone passing through, especially backpackers.
(If you are not one, you will probably be treated better, but that does not prevent you from boycotting either.)

I am a 28-year-old woman, polite and respectful. This is the first time I have found myself facing someone who deliberately had no intention of letting things go well, who provoked until obtaining a reaction in order to then exclude someone “properly.”

Not all backpackers are exemplary — as everywhere in the world.
But stigmatizing a group and fueling contempt is never acceptable.

I needed to leave a written record of what happened here.
Because it is serious.
And because silence always protects bad practices.
Great little campsite. Clean amenities & good camp kitchen. Unpowered, 2 adults, $30 per night

Quiet, easy to find and only a few minutes walk from a couple of Cafe bakeries, gift shops and local museum. A nearby beautifully landscaped new park is the starting point for a walk around this lovely little historical village.

Great location. Reception is hard to find. It’s 50 meters back down the road you just came in - why they don’t have a sign at reception is a little strange. Every person who arrives has the same look of confusions. Small park and facilities are ok - clean. Short walk into town or to visit the historic bridge. Staff were friendly.
Amazing little caravan park, right on the river. Close to shops and wonderful walks. Manager is super friendly.

I was at this site last week during the festive season. Maybe my experience was different from others because it was a busy period, but unfortunately we didn’t have a good experience with the shared kitchen.

The kitchen was dirty, and the extension plugs were placed right beside the cooker, which felt unsafe. The cooker and oven were not well maintained and dirty. The fridge was so full that we couldn’t even put our own food inside. The plates in the cabinet were dusty and rusty, and the cabinet itself was also dusty.

There was no proper cooker, only a 2-in-1 cooker on top of the oven, and it heated up very slowly, making cooking difficult. We ended up throwing away our food and eating instant noodles during the festive season instead.

Overall, the place feels rundown. The only good thing is that the toilets were clean and well maintained, which we appreciated.
Just stayed 1 night and it was perfect for the price.
Emily and the team presented a beautiful room in the old tannery in the park. Thank you for a very warm and comfortable experience
A great place to stop between Devonport and Hobart.

Worse owner ever- caravan place was pretty empty and came to ask at 9pm to move just « in case » someone came in later (highly unlikely the place would fill up at this time of year). Not friendly at all
Dog friendly and right in the centre of town. Plenty of bare sites. Powered sites get snapped up. Amenities block is lovely.
Stayed in caravan park with a powered site.  Message said they were closed for maintenance but they aren't. Lovely little caravan park with clean and tidy amenities.  Great friendly staff.
Beautiful little camp set beside the river and the service is friendly and helpful. Beautiful walks and loads of local history to discover.
Great place to stay, close to all local attractions. Manager of facility is fantastic couldnt be more friendly and helpful.
Lovely park in the middle of a fantastic town. The reception staff very helpful.
Well layed out. Great ammenities. Very friendly hosts. This is our third stay here. Will return.
Nice small campground, good hot showers and clean toilet. Power and water. Handy location.

Sweet little caravan park right on the river, within easy walking distance of town & all the historic attractions. Emily was super friendly & helpful. Amenities more than adequate. Enjoyed our visit to this dear little spot.

What a bonzer caravan park .
Amenities were clean and ample and the caretaker was great to deal and chat with considering she runs the place on her own . We'd recommend staying here to explore the area 😃
El pueblo es un encanto y los espacios para acampar están bien equipados, limpios y bien atendidos. Vale la pena
We spent 2 nights at the caravan park recently and couldn't fault it . The manager was very friendly and helpful and we'll be back

Wonderful camp site.
For $32  per night, I had unlimited power, steaming showers,  and a well equipped camp kitchen. The site was clean, the facilities clean and the staff were friendly and helpful. There were a few other campers but it was quiet and peaceful.
I'd recommend 100%
Nice  short walk to some of the best coffee around as well.
Value for money. Picturesque spot by river and Ross Bridge. Easy walk to town centre.
The facilities are great here. Even I arrive late, the staff is nice to help me when I called her. I enjoy staying here.
What a great place. Emily thought of everything. A lovely place with everything you could need for a comfortable night. Beautiful sandstone cabin.
Vibes are immaculate. Highly recommend
Good park right in the midst of the gorgeous heritage village
This is a lovely park by the river.  The people are friendly and the amenities are very good too.  I would recommend this park to everyone.
Great little caravan park. The toilet block has just been updated.
100% we will be back here, felt very welcomed

One of the nicest little campsites we have had the pleasure of staying at. We camped on beautiful green grass, clean amenities and a cosy little camp kitchen. Historic sites are right across the road and there is a lovely bakery/eatery within walking distance.

We have just stayed a week at the Ross caravan park, staff were very friendly, amenities were great,clean and well maintained, great camp kitchen could not ask for anything more. Fantastic place to do some exploring such as the  Convict Female  Factory, the Sandstone Quarry,  Church and the  famous Ross Bridge.The sandstone quarry is where the stone was cut and used in a lot of buildings  around the area. |Good mixture of stores, antiques, gifts, wool store, coffee shops, bakery and Pub. The birdlife on the river and sunsets are remarkable, so do yourself a favor and put Ross on your bucket list.

We stayed for 8 nights over Easter so it was very busy, but still worked out well. It's a good small park in a great location.    Amenities all good. Admin side of things still needs some fine tuning but helpful and friendly

We were in an RV in May and really enjoyed our stay. We ended up extending our stay a night due to some insane winds we weren’t keen to drive in with a high profile vehicle. The owners were very friendly and accommodating. Grounds are flat and amenities exceptional - a full undercover camp kitchen, very nice shower & toilet facilities and great location next to The famous Ross Bridge. The town is lovely with beautiful convict-built sandstone buildings, tree-lined street and and a nice museum at the Tasmanian Wool Centre in town. In May lots of places were closed Monday and Tuesday (including the pub) but the owners made that clear in the info sent before arrival. Ross is a little step back in time as your travel through the midlands of Tasmania. The caravan park would be very crowded in summer I think. They also have cute little cottages on site.
We enjoyed our 5 night stay here. We were there over the ANZAC period & loved the Dawn Service.

We were only there for one day, but it was enough to realize that the place is run by very professional and dedicated people. The staff is very friendly and provides accurate information. The campsite is beautiful, the kitchen is well-equipped, the bathroom is very clean, and it even has heating. Highly recommended.

Our camping group stayed at Ross caravan park over Easter 2025 Park and amenities  were very clean and tidy BUT the park manager was the rudest person I have come across my wife was polity  about to ask a question and was shut down and told she is not important  and don't matter so don't speak then my mate asked about being parked under trees and are they safe and was told to show some respect so lady we are important  and do matter  and we are respectful  and we are your customers and you need a lesson in customer relations it's park managers like yourself that turn tourists  away from these lovely towns .No rating from me and don't go there.
Clean & tidy caravan park, toilets clean & tidy. Quiet, not really a caravan park to take children, as there is no playground.  Walking distance to the small Town of Ross.
Great little park in a pretty little town easy to spend a few days exploring the area Managers are friendly and amenities are always clean
Nice spacious room with a kitchenette and modern bathroom
No stars - definitely don't stay here. There's no service whatsoever, we had to walk down to the public toilets. Despite paying we were never given any parking details or assistance.

Ross is a beautiful place that has managed to maintain its history and it’s a credit to all those involved. Just beware of the booking process at the van park as mentioned elsewhere in recent reviews. Best to call and clarify expectations.

Nice caravan park with great amenities. A very peaceful and tranquil corner of Ross. Scenic walking trail along a lake and a town of historic significance. Lots of photo-taking opportunities in the area and enjoyed the tranquil early morning hikes. Happy to have stopped by on our campervan trip from Launceston to Hobart. Thank you very much for the hospitality.

We loved our 12 day stay in this Caravan Park, in the very historic town of Ross.
The manager and staff were very friendly, helpful and attentive.

The whole park and amenities were kept clean and tidy.

Easy walk to all the local attractions, and food places.

And easy drive to a vast number of great historic locations in the general area.

We had a campervan and the location was great. We could walk everywhere from the park and explore the town and surrounding areas. Very nice facilities. Great pub over the road also within walking distance. Very much recommend staying here as there is a lot to do in this lovely little town.

I booked this park months in advance & Emily was very helpful. The caretaker too was so helpful.
The park is small & gets very busy, having said that it is quiet & very well kept. The amenities are small & cleaned daily.
The park is within walking distance to the cute little town of Ross & about an hour to Launceston.
The Ross bridge is certainly worth a look & take a photo on sunset.
If only I  could give Management no stars,  no professionalism about her. Would not stay there again.

The tannery room was very good but I also encountered the dreadful customer service from the officious and incompetent 'manager' mentioned in other reviews here..  Despite request at the time of check in and twice more during the stay, then two email and one phone call follow -up, I have been unable to get a simple receipt for the money I paid so I can claim reimbursement. The excuses are hilarious - she could not provide a printed invoice because there was no ink in the printer, then the payment system would not let her send a duplicate receipt, then it must be the fault of my email system. And my phone calls were an inconvenience because she was too busy cleaning.||The pretty town of Ross deserves a better ambassador than Emily
We stayed in a room in the tannery.  Great renovation,  so comfortable and quirky.  Ross is gorgeous and you can explore on foot from here.

Location and facilities very good. However our experience with Emily, the manager was less than desirable and downright rude.She seemed to be more interested in getting our money rather than providing a hospitable, friendly service. The actual price she charged was more than what was advertised with no explanation and no access codes to the facilities were provided. When asked to clarify these matters she called us rude and asked us to leave even though it was late in the afternoon. In all my years of travelling I am bewildered to be treated in such a manner. Despite this parks location I would not recommend it to anyone.

Ross Brown
Lennox Head, NSW
10.3.25

Rang 8 days before the long weekend to make booking for 2 days. Then rang up the day before arriving to confirm that we were booked in.  We were told not a problem, we have a site for you and the check in time is 12pm. When we arrived we were told politely they had NO site booked for us, telling us that she had taken a lot of calls and didn't remember our conversation. We had to find a site elsewhere.
NOT HAPPY.
Quiet location and just a stone's throw from the bridge.

There were so many positives which were then let down by some fairly inconsistent customer service.  I stayed in the ‘Tannery Rooms’ at the caravan park.  They were cheap and perfect for a one or two night stay.  They look recently renovated with a great bathroom and small kitchen.  Compact room but had everything you needed.  The day of checkin there was even a text advising that local pub would be closed but a food van would be in town.  They even provided the menu which I thought was a great touch.  |On checkin I was asked for a $10 key deposit.  The lady behind the counter said they had a lot of trouble with keys and the $10 was to make sure it was returned.  Whilst I understand, it didn’t make sense when I saw what appeared to be key boxes at each room so they could have been left there.  I asked what time reception was open in the morning and she said, just call, I will be here.|I went to checkout at 8.45am.  There was no-one at reception so I called the number.  I was then told she wasn’t on site and would be back in 5 mins.  I was also asked if I had paid a key deposit (not sure why some people did and others did not).  I waited with 2 other people who were also waiting to check out (and I’d seen two other couples in the Tannery Rooms packing up).  There were obviously quite a few people checking out that day and she went off site before 9am.  I waited almost 10 mins and when she turned up she had a takeaway coffee in her hand, advising she went to ‘feed the chickens’ and mentioned how as soon as she leaves people turn up.  Considering it was before 10am and everyone hadn’t gone yet, I’m not surprised people were waiting to check out.  I’m not sure if it was deliberate to get people to leave without their key deposits?

超乾淨的露營區，設備很齊全，東西都維護的很好，不管是廚房還是洗澡的地方，真的都超乾淨，廚房有微波爐、冰箱、電磁爐還有一張桌子，如果自己沒有設備的來這個露營區，也可以玩得很開心哦。
還可以洗衣服跟曬衣場，露營區後面就是Ross Bridge，下次再回到Ross一定也會選擇這個露營區，真的超棒的
By a nice river, shady spots, decent rates and lovely service. Amenities very clean and well cared for. Spots for tents and self contained vehicles.
Nice small park with very clean amenities. Easy walking distance to the village and the historic sites

CAREFUL WITH ONLINE BOOKING!
We booked our site online and selected 9pm as our arrival time.

When we arrived at 8:15pm the Check-In was closed, the number it said to call didn‘t pick up and the phone number on the website ignored my calls and whatsapp messages.
Only when my girlfriend called the number 45min later she got a call back. When the lady heard my name (that we booked on), she got VERY pissed and told us how dear we arrive so late and that this is unacceptable.

Turns out you need to arrive before 6pm, even though you can select a much later arrival time online…..
And if you text or e-mail the owner with your booking name after 6pm you will be ghosted.

Otherwise its quite nice here, the toilets are clean and the river is right next to the campsite…
Pretty decent albeit basic caravan park. Good value for money. Amenities have been recently renovated and were very clean when we visited. The town is a nice place to visit too.

This is a favourite little park in the heart of Historic Ross on the Heritage Highway Tasmania. Our family have decided to visit annually in January as the local pool is open for the kids, the caravan park  is safe for kids too. Across the road is a little park and in the main street another park for the kids to play at.  The streets are quiet after 5 pm so bike riding is safe.  Great local Hotel for meals when open.|Emmenities including a great little camp kitchen are clean and tidy.  The attached units are very comfortable to if you don't want to camp.|All in all we recommend this camp-site
Good place to overnight and explore the small town the next morning.

After two months travelling Tasmania, this caravan park is the one to come to for a HOT shower!!! And your not charged for it. Lovely little park, amenities block just painted inside and out, don’t be put off because it's old. New manager is very customer focused 👌

Ross is a lovely old town in Tasmania. A nice place to visit, with the Tasmanian Wool Museum, the ruins of the old 'Female Factory' (Women convict incarceration), and lovely wide, clean laid out streets.||||However. The council owned, but privately leased Ross Caravan Park, is to be avoided. ||||Unless you enjoy, or can easily put up with a small-minded, petty, Anal Rule Enforcing, Park Manager. She huffs and puffs and makes you move, whether or not you are already set up, change angle, points out to you where you are breaking the caravan park Rules, which, as everybody knows, have been drafted and need an Enforcer.||||The park, when our confrontation took place at 7am, necessitating a pack up and couple up, to move the caravan forward by 3 meters, as we were set up in site 14, and not site 13. The park has only got four caravans in it, and no-one within cooee of us. Sites are not marked out, including a supposed 'road' included in the grass, marked only by three with dots. When asked at what time all the other bookings were arriving, necessitating our being told to move, she reverted to her Mantra of 'There are Rules, and we have to abide by the Rules.' One look at the Park handout, shows only 'You must...' and not 'Please would you...'||||At my stage of life (74) I do not need to have my day upset by a peanut-brained individual, whose self worth is obviously scaffolded on her petty mindedness.||||The park is advertised online, as Big Rig Friendly. NOT. On another site*, in this almost empty park, a bus RV and small trailer overlapped the length of their site by 1.5m. They were made to pay for two sites. In an empty park.||||Campbell Town is close - they have a wonderful free parking RV site. Kempton is close, and even better I am told.||||We will be heading there, 2 days short of our booking at Ross, whether or not she refunds our fees.||My advice, boycott this park, and visit Ross from other venues.||||David and Jo Neilson
We loved this town and the caravan park rooms were really comfy
Very friendly staff , very tidy park + amenates clean .
We stayed here in our Van its  a small park but very clean and tidy. The caretaker was so very helpful.
Great place to stay for a few days or a the weekend.
A relatively cheap and pleasant place to pop your caravan. Friendly staff and good amenities.

Always great and friendly service. Very willing to accommodate special requests in a no fuss manner. We stay here every year during the Ross running festival and it's super convenient being only a very short walk to the start and finish line.
Amazing cabin, and the staff was genuinely one of the nicest people that i have ever met. cannot praise this caravan park enough. Highly recommend!

Our accommodation was spacious, cosy, clean & well appointed. The weather did not dampen our enthusiasm!! We love Ross… the history, the friendly locals, the scallop pies from the bakery and the meal at the pub were excellent. Will definitely stay again.

The cabins are great , warm and cosy .
Very welcoming service
Great value in a central position in the town, all within walking distance.

Perfect caravan park with powered and unpowered site options, very cleaned amenities such as toilet, shower, and kitchen with BBQ, electric kettle, microwave, fridge, etc. Etc.
Customer service was amazing. And location wise also good if you are coming from Devonport and heading to Hobart the  must stay on this caravan site.

What an absolutely lovely experience in our caravan!
We hadn't planned to stay in Ross but from word of mouth, from a fellow QLD, who told us about this little gem, hit the spot amazingly for our family!
Walking distance to the bakery, little lolly shop and the hotel.
Amenities were clean and tidy and the washer and dryer coin operated but also able to use your phone to pay (just an added bonus if you run out of coins). The stunning bridge is also walking distance with lovers lane as a cute surprise.
Sites are big and smaller, whatever you need for your rig. Space for the children to run around and play, with the peaceful serenity to enjoy the evening. Camp kitchen well equipped as well.
Manger on-site so easy to communicate with and passionate about keeping it simple and meeting the heritage of the place.
Can't wait to visit again!
Very accommodating care-takers.  Great camp kitchen and good showers!!
Cabins- Modern, clean rooms. Friendly service. Very peaceful, quiet and secluded.

We stayed here for one night in a self contained caravan.
It was a clean tidy park and right by the river and historic Bridge. Everything is within walking distance.
The new owners were really friendly, helpful and approachable.
Thank you.
Lovely self-contained cabins that are beautifully decorated and cleaned to a high standard. The pricing is very ggod value.

We loved it here. Stayed in one of the cottages. All facilities available, super camp kitchen....great management,soo friendly.... nothing too much trouble. Beautiful little heritage town with great bakeries and old pub.... loads of old beautful buildings  ,excellent, and great value! Thanks so much!

We had a large caravan which Mike helped accommodate. We had the intention to stay the night and ended up staying 3 nights instead, cause the not only the town was beautiful, the service from both Mike and Nick was fantastic, but the location was absolutely amazing.

Caravan park is in a great location, just a short stroll to the bridge, bakeries, take away store and the pub that does good meals. Very friendly and helpful staff, level sites. Toilet block basic but very clean, used small laundry which had everything needed.|Only drawback was being woken by the noise of a train hooting in the night one night, but otherwise a great stay!

Beautiful park, well kept and friendly.  Not expensive for powered sites spent 2 nights there.  Had dinner at pub, great food not expensive. Go to the bakery for awesome vanilla slice. Lots of history and easy walking

A perfect place to stop with your motor home/caravan in Ross. The caravan park is right beside the river, next to the famous bridge and only a minute's walk to the shops in town.
Mike, the manager, was very lovely and helpful. The toilets and showers were spacious and very clean. The cooking area is a nice space with bbq, hot plates, microwave, toasters and kettles. Would definitely recommend staying here and looking around Ross, which is a beautiful and historic town.

Small but amazing caravan park located next to the famous Ross Bridge and stroll into the town centre . The manager was very helpful and welcoming. The camp kitchen was well organised with table and chairs. A most relaxing park with good shady areas.
Such a great little caravan park in a lovely location. Being looked after really well by Michael and Sharon the current Managers.

Manger , Mike, could not have been more helpful in accommodating our group of six touring cyclists. The cabins are cosy, clean and comfortable. The park is clean and quiet , suitable for families and those wanting a peaceful stay. The location near the historic Ross bridge is lovely and the hotel and bakeries are a few minutes walk away. The camp kitchen and washing amenities were great. Highly recommended.
Nice clean park , great people and a quaint town .

Great little van park. One of the only ones we have stayed and all there washing machines and dryers are working. And only $4.😁 the guys went above and beyond  as they didnt have a site big enough so joined 2 smaller ones so we could fit. Very funny layout though, could get more vans in with a redesign and they need it as it was booked out midweek.

We had two nights at ross on our trip around Tas and the ross caravan park was the perfect place to camp we had a large unpowered site use of the camp kitchen free showers amenities were kept spotless Mike  the manager was the perfect host very accommodating he made the stay better  thanks  for everything Mike keep up the good work . Highly recommend a stay at ross caravan park .
Very pleasaant place to stay, close to town and great situation. Clean amenities, sites are flat with plenty of room and very friendly, helpful staff.
Nice place will be back. Highly recommend

Had a great stay at Ross Caravan Park. Very warm welcome from Mike. Room is great size, clean and very comfy bed. All great amenities for weekend getaway stay. Perfect spot to stopover between Launceston and Freycinet. Ross is a lovely and quiet town. Will definitely be back again.
Bonnie
Friendly staff, very good amenities, nice location, excellent all around
Great friendly reception, good clean amenities. Free hot showers. Washing and drying facilities coin operated. Nice well kept quiet park. Highly recommended.

Really great value for money. The camp kitchen has all the required amenities (oven, stove, microwave, bbq, fridge etc) in a really friendly communal area. It's more suited towards travellers without children which is a great thing for those travelling without that want a quieter place to stay.

We stayed here and travelled out to Coles bay and it was a great midway location for day trips.
No taps in the kitchen or laundry have hot water but you can easily work around this with the provided kettles.

Only downside is it is rather close to a train line that you will hear honking when it passes through town throughout the night

As we swag it, Wanderer ing across the beautiful Tasmania a stop in Ross was in order.

A fantastic little town and camping spot.
I kove this place. Laid back and peaceful. So much history here

We loved our stay at Ross Caravan Park. Mike was so welcoming, helpful and made sure we had everything we needed. We were blown away by how clean the amenities were, they were absolutely spotless and the camp kitchen had everything we needed. Beautiful little spot on the river, we wished we could have stayed longer.

We have been in Tassie for 10 nights lots of bookings needed to be made for our motor home holiday. The communication from Nick and Mike was next level from confirming our booking, sending a reminder with great information about what to expect and our wonderful warm welcome. The park was spotless, the camp kitchen well organised and clean. We had a wonderful stay. Thank you so much guys. Keep doing what you are doing. I will definitely book with you again if I have the fortune of coming back but in the meantime I cannot recommend you and your wonderful friendly little town enough.
Great little caravan park in a beautiful town, well worth a visit and walk around. Park is well maintained and clean, everything you need with great and very friendly service! Highly recommended.

Our hosts Sharon and Mike were so lovely and got us a room ready in 30 minutes for a late walk-in, and for this thank you so much !! The room was just perfect and spotless, everything you need was in here, again thank you !

We were welcomed so warmly by Mike and offered a choice of heritage cabins. What a wonderful job has been done in renovating and extending these historic dwellings. They are roomy, comfortable, spotlessly clean, light filled and warmed by a very efficient wall panel heater when the evening draws in cold. They are obviously much loved and it shows in the care taken in maintenance and cleaning. To top it off the nightly tariff is a steal almost, compared to many other Tassie properties we have used.....excellent value for money. You won't regret this gorgeous stop over.

A very nice et quiet.caravan park. Mike the manager is very friendly and helped us a lot to have the most pleasant stay possible.  I recommend this caravan Park without any hesitation.  Thank you again Mike for your welcome.
Good areas for parking, no concrete but firm ground, very close to centre of town and pub.
Very well situated in the center of town

Ross is a very interesting place to visit with a significant historic background halfway on Launceston-Hobart road. As such the campvan is very well located and easy to find close to the center. Suited and clean amenities with a lot of options. Thanks a lot to the manager and his assistant for their warm welcome and for being very helpful when things are going wrong ! We recommend that location.
We camped here for 2 nights in October. Fantastic location, close to everything and its an amazing village. The caretakers are warm and friendly and it's a well run park. Plus has good WiFi!
Great little caravan park had everything we needed and the managers were excellent hosts even providing a delicious spread for the Halloween happy hour.
Very welcoming reception from Michael. Fabulous position walking distance to everything.
Michael and his wife were very helpful and friendly.. did everything possible to make our stay memorable
Great cabin for a stopover. friendly and helpful people .
Couldn't find a nicer spot to Stay .Caretaker's were absolutely 💯 % genuine lovely People. Please stop and spend a day or 2 .you won't regret it .They gave me so much needed personal support ...

Lovely little caravan park with very kind hosts, Mike and Sharon
who went above and beyond to make sure we were looked after. We cannot thank them enough.
The facilities were very clean and warm, with free showers and piping hot water, a great camp kitchen with everything you could need.
The park is just behind the main street, and very flat, perfect for my friend who has a disability.
We both have dogs, and they were made very welcome too
We can't wait to go back!!

Great council Caravan park, power/water $32 a night for 2…
150m from main town square and bakery…
Great facilities, clean and quiet location.
Definitely be back stayed 3 nights.

Mike was great to deal with. Very nice guy and they run a great caravan park. Amenities are always extremely clean. He even put our annex up when it was windy to avoid damage. Beautiful location in the heart of town ! After two weeks can Highly recommend 👌
Wonderful location. Very clean amenities. Hosts very helpful and friendly

It is one of the most beautiful tiny Caravan Parks, A GEM. Perfect located for exploring the town and area, walking distance to the town points of interest, perfectly maintained, clean and cosy facilities, dog friendly: A GEM!!!!!!! Mike the manager was professional and caring that everybody is happy and comfortable. Luna my dog loved it too, the walk along the river it was a delight. Thank you!!!!!
Rang Mike from on the road, desperate for a bed for the night, nothing else along the way. Mike was so gracious and helpful we were so grateful. The room was fantastic and comfortable, thanks Mike 😊
A beautiful town. Great caravannpark owners. Large sites. Strongly recommend.

We just love this little caravan park. Nice and quiet during the cooler months and because everything is so close you can stay hooked up. The facilities are always clean, as is the camp kitchen which has everything you might need. There is a new communal fire pit or you can have your own above ground fire. Mike is a fantastic host - nothing is too much trouble. We will definitely be back!
Great location, old buildings. History everywhere.

It was winter when we stayed and the weather wasnt the best. The ammenities could do with a heater.
Very small park in need of some love.
Easy walk to town.

First time staying since leonie and James stopped running the place, new caretaker friendly and has alot of plans to upgrade things but council won't give money, beautiful little dog kept on lead which is great to see that they are keeping to the rules, never had a bad experience staying here so peaceful, relaxing with our firepot, cute little historic town unsure of previous reviews those people are nutters free hot showers clean and respectful Mel and maxie Hobart tas

Well maintained cabin rooms, great option if you want to stay in the cooler weather or don't want to set up tents. Handy to see they've upgraded to add bathrooms in the cabins since I was last there!

Supplies in the cabins are great, has all the basics. The camp kitchen out with the outdoor barbecues even has a cupboard with some basic supplies.

The care takers were very nice and accommodating.

This park is well run by caretakers Mike and Sharon with their little dog.  I've stayed here 4 times in the last 2 months and have always found Mike and Sharon to be friendly, helpful and just lovely people. New to caravanning Mike helped me onto my site, was respectful and very helpful. I felt very safe here and hence why I keep coming back. Amenities are clean, the park is walking distance to town, cafes, pub. A beautiful little town and Caravan Park.  Highly recommend and I will definitely be back again. Thanks Mike and Sharon for looking after my little dog and me! You are awesome.😄

We just keep coming back. Central to this wonderful town of bakeries and hotel.  Managers Mike and Sharon go out of their way to assist. The caravan park  is spotless. We see their tiny friendly dog on his daily walks ( on lead) always ready for a hello and pat. Showers are hot with great pressure and no coins required. Laundry payment via coin or credit card - again spotless and well maintained. A credit to the managers.
Great spot to stop over on way to cradle mountain. Very nice town with amazing night photo opportunities.

Fantastic spot, I enjoyed staying there.  The manager was very accommodating.  Value for money on a powered site.  Very clean facilities.  I stayed there longer than I anticipated, which was great 😊.  Their dog Sophie is a cute fur ball, the size of a cat, (not a guard dog size at all, if you read two previous reviews).  As a female solo traveller, I felt safe and comfortable, no issues whatsoever.  Well worth visiting.  Thanks to Mike and Sharon, cheers, Rose Gray (not Swan anymore).
Stayed in the cabin simply beautiful and top people looking after me

horrible place to stay. i think this bloke has just taken over the place recently, wasn’t like this last time i stayed here, he has a vicious guard dog, that we couldn’t get close enough to the reception to complain, everything was untidy and said was cleaned daily, no hot water in the showers. I’m sure we caught him looking through our window as well. Would NOT take my children there, my wife felt very unsafe and had to lock the doors every night!!!!

Awful experience! Owner is an absolute creep, had a big scary guard dog, very vicious, he was peeking through caravan windows most nights, , didn’t feel safe around him, I would b carefull taking kids there, wouldn’t rate this experience to anyone!! Definitely better places to stay in Tasmania
